title: copernicia berteroana
intro: Copernicia berteroana, also known as the 'Palmillo', is a type of palm tree native to Cuba and the Dominican Republic. Its trunk bark has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and treating skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the bark is typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or ointments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
